review_title: Great ease-of-use and customer centricity
description: We have been able to scale operational reporting and decision making because of Holistics
author: Amrt S.
pros: Easy to setup Support for MongoDB SQL based dashboarding Granular access permission
cons: NA

review_title: Versatile, user-friendly BI tool for simplifying daily data operations
description: Holistics is a powerful data visualization tool that automates reporting and analytics. With its flexible querying capabilities and seamless integration, it speeds up work by up to 70%. The user-friendly interface makes it easy to use, enabling faster and more accurate decision-making. A highly useful solution for data-driven teams.
author: Hoang Duc H.
pros: Useful features: 1. Data Modeling: Use for large data model without using complex queries. 2. Shareable Links: Easy to share reports within Departments/Clients.
cons: 1. Cannot combine 2.0 report into 3.0 dashboard E.g: Some report needs to use complex query to visualize but we can't create it in a 3.0 dashboard, force us to create new table in dataset 2. Job dependency: Reports cannot load independent therefore with multiple users use holistics at a same time, they will need to wait for their turn.

review_title: Emailing Feature
description: We have built an app for Construction domain. So they are overall paper-based industry. the CEO who sits in HO, has no visibility of what is happening in different sites. Now with this dashboard, their 60% of work is automated and it has 100% visibility and tracking is effective. Now mistakes are reduced, project is on track. One email every mrng gives PM a large pic of his project
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 1. The best feature is of this software is, scheduling the automatic email, which is the number 1 request from all our customers. If one day holistics email stops, our customers will question us. 2. Email feature gives the Top management like CEO, PM's, Director the overall view of what is happening at their project. 3. Best is Dashboard Emailing - 200% benefit, Even microsoft Power BI doesnot have this feature, so you guys beat Power BI in this aspect. Kudos. 4. Cant imagine a day without your software.
cons: 1. The major drawback is Drilldown feature not available like Power BI 2. Creating Timeseries filters, dropdown takes more time in holistics. Which needs improvements. 3. Very less interactive. 4. When emailed large dashboard with wider bar graph, few bar lines in that gets shrinked and creates confusion for users. It would be much better if you could address this better.

review_title: Excellent self-service BI tool for building a golden source of truth in large organizations.
description: After evaluating over 40 BI tools, we chose Holistics for its robust semantic modeling layer, which enabled us to create a centralized source of truth for our ~1,000 users. While it took time for our team to adjust to the modeling-first mindset, the benefits have been transformative. We now have a reliable framework for data logic that fosters collaboration and ensures accuracy across all dashboards. Features like cross-filtering and drill-through functionality have made insights much more accessible and actionable. Holistics has been a reliable partner in helping us scale our data operations and transition to a self-service analytics model. The platform’s flexibility, consistency, and technical depth make it a valuable addition to our tech stack, enabling us to grow while maintaining data accuracy and efficiency.
author: Juan A.
pros: Centralized, reusable analytics through a code-based semantic modeling layer. Robust, flexible self-service features for business users. Robust geographical mapping capabilities. Good variety of charts. Intuitive exploration features like cross-filtering and drill-through. Transparent, scalable pricing. Strong administrative features with Git version control, RBAC, and usage monitoring. Responsive team with a customer-first mentality.
cons: The initial learning curve for teams transitioning from SQL. Legacy 3.0 dashboards have traceability challenges, while newer Canvas-based options have exploration limitations that limit broader adoption.

review_title: Great product which keeps improving
description: We switched from PowerBI to Holistics for reporting needs about a year ago and have been thrilled with the choice. It's much easier to securely share data with third parties via password-protected shareable links. Integrating alerts via slack has been a breeze. The pricing is also quite reasonable for everything offered.
author: Adam H.
pros: The platform is built for flexibility and constantly evolving. The [sensitive content hidden] genuinely solicit user feedback and incorporate it into their roadmap.
cons: There are a few UI difficulties. These are minor, but can be a bit frustrating at times.

review_title: Enjoying our experience with Holistics after 3 months
description: We are still actively building data models and creating reporting dashboards, and we are hopeful that Holistics will be more user friendly to non-technical users, which would be a huge plus for our organization.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We identified Holistics to replace Chartio as our data analysis and visualization platform after Chartio was discontinued as a result of acquisition. We have been using Holistics for about 3 months, and our experience is largely positive. It was easy to connect with the data sources, and there is a lot of flexibility how the analysts can configure the data via the data modeling layer as we need. When we have questions, whether it is more general about the product or a more specific feature, we have been able to get assistance from the Support team in a timely manner, which is really good for migrating to a new product.
cons: In general we would like to see more ability to customize the reports on the visualization side. There are also some issues when loading the reports and refreshing the page often helps.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Holistic an amazing analytics solution
description: Awesome team!!! They work with you and great leadership.
author: Praveen C.
pros: Simple elegant and most easiest tool. When we started this entire team onboarding was so smooth and with minimum effort we were able to pickup and become pro in this
cons: It satisfied our requirements but like every solution even holistic has opportunity to improve and one thing I would like to see is in implementing drill down information.

review_title: Great product, easy to use, excellent pricing
description: I've had a good experience with Holistics, the customer support is excellent, they are constantly building up new functions (the latest being Git integration, which is super important for version control).
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to implement Good pricing structure and scalable to meet our needs ongoing Has some really great features for the price - semantic layer, report scheduler (Slack and email), drillthrough reporting SQL based for report developers Data exploration is simple for non technical business users (drag and drop reporting) I just think it's a really solid product and great for a modern data stack
cons: No custom visualizations (although the standard ones available are likely to meet most requirements)
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Scalable, Code-Driven BI Platform for Advanced Analytics
description: Holistics has completely changed the way we create business dashboards from our data. It’s so much faster than relying on ad-hoc tools, and it makes building and maintaining dashboards a lot more efficient. The tool fits perfectly with our engineering philosophy of using code-based tools to ensure everything is reproducible and easy to maintain. With features like analytics-as-code and Git integration, managing our BI stack has become more streamlined, whether for internal reporting or delivering customer-facing analytics.
author: Jose R.
pros: Its Git-based version control allows us to bulk edit, refactor and maintain our dashboards more efficiently. It’s a massive time-saver. Common analytical functions, such as period-over-period comparisons and percent-of-total calculations, are natively supported as built-in features, which significantly reduce our development time. The platform also performs well even as our data needs and transaction volumes grow.
cons: Some self-service features could use a bit of polishing since non-technical users occasionally run into UX bumps.

review_title: Intuitive and Empowering Self-Service BI
description: I rely on Holistics daily to monitor performance and access insights. The platform is intuitive, easy to use, offering clear dashboards and the ability to quickly find the data I need.
author: Andrew B.
pros: 1) Easy to use: I can check my key metrics daily, spot trends, and use the dashboards for presentations to our stakeholders. 2) Interactive, flexible dashboards: It’s really easy to add filters and drill down into the dashboard to get insights for specific campaigns. The ability to customize views makes it really convenient for me to focus on specific data.
cons: There are minor UX improvements needed, for example, filter placement within the search box can clutter the experience.

review_title: Holistics Brings Governed Insights to Our Entire Organization
description: Holistics dashboards have become an integral part of our daily routine at ARD to help with timely decision-making. Five years back, there were only a few teams having access to data, and it was like having a door you should step through to access the data. So not everyone could go through that door because they didn't have the key for it. But right now with tools like Holistics, everyone has the key to unlock the right doors, and via the dashboards, they can access important information about the platform's usage and about how users interact with our content.
author: Johanna G.
pros: Holistics is easy to use and intuitive, making it easy for our business users to quickly get value out of it. We love the drag-and-drop options for building reports and exploring data. Additionally, the ability to add business calculations when creating charts offers great flexibility as we have more possibilities to change data and to add formulas to make a more precise chart. Holistics access control is also really robust. We have a huge user base with diverse user groups with varying requirements and tasks, and Holistics makes it really simple to share different dashboards with different groups without losing track. We also had the chance this year to try out the newer version of Holistics Canvas dashboard recently, which offers more design options, resulting in great new charts and dashboards! We have taken our dashboards to a new level of design. Their support team is also incredible, always offering enthusiastic and quick responses to our questions.
cons: While Canvas dashboard is great, it’s still in early beta so creating dashboards have become slightly more complex. However, the Holistics team has shared with us that they are well-aware of it and actively improving the user experience. We've encountered performance issues with lengthy dashboards, which occasionally cause out-of-memory errors. While the autosave feature has helped mitigate some of these problems, we sometimes face interruptions due to the sheer size of our dashboards.

review_title: Holistics is an excellent choice!
description: We have built visualizations in Holistics that took it way beyond the 'out-of-the-box' tools. WE are exploring the embedding of the advanced custom charts and have been quite successful. Overall, once we formulated our framework and truly understood the requirements for preventing circular relationships, we have found it easy to work in and very effective at communicating what information really resides within our data.
author: Seth L.
pros: We are a startup with a limited budget and, after a review of our options, Holistics became an excellent choice to get us going in BI without the typical huge annual expense. The team at Holistics immediately responded to one of my requests for an enhancement to the tool before we had signed our first contract. Incredible! I have not ever had that kind of customer service. It is a powerful tool for a reasonable price from a great company.
cons: We need the ability to build an interface with guardrails for our less tech-savvy users and there is no way to build a custom landing page as of yet. We would also like the ability to copy a report between data sources. Export and import of the metadata would suffice.

review_title: Great fit for fast-paced teams
description: Overall, Holistics is great for fast-paced teams. It enables us to quickly get and deliver data to consumers without spending extensive amounts of time on development and support of this process.
author: Stanislav L.
pros: Holistics is easy to adopt and use as it is web-based. No client installation, user licenses management, additional dedicated infrastructure requirements or other clutter like that.Data sources are easy to connect, and once connection is set you are ready to create data models and visualization on top of them.Data models are built using SQL which is great for us, as it is commonly used by our development team.Holistics rolls out updates regularly and their support team is responsive and quick.
cons: Variety of supported visualization options is not super extensive (Holistics plans to add custom built visualization functionality, which should solve this though)

review_title: Easy and intuitive for non technical users
author: Jing Yu L.
pros: The drag and drop functionality makes it easy for non-data users to learn, and eventually create their own reports for exploration and analysis - the "fan out" errors are a great way to alert users if they are potentially making a mistake. The modelling layer forces analysts to think about how to reduce overlaps in code (i.e. reusing models across datasets). I really like the "matches" date filter operator, as it reduces the number of clicks and mental processing required from users during selection.
cons: New table columns cannot be automatically detected in table models, and have to be manually added via AML. There is also a trade off in the complexity of self-serve if too many relationships are defined in a dataset. It would be great if there are more advanced access control features and version control features (e.g. creation of pull requests).

review_title: Very useful SaaS BI/Data modeling platform
author: Albert C.
pros: For us, it was very convenient that business users where able to create their own dashboards using Holistics web app, instead of having to use a desktop software and then viewing them in the cloud. From the data engineering team we would prepare the data models (a la DBT, using SQL, very useful!) and datasets and business users would use them.
cons: In the beginning the SQL queries used to create de data models could not be hosted outside Holistics (Githug, for example), that meant that code versioning and history were not really available. I know that this is changing in the new versions of Holistics-as-code (haven't been able to try them yet!)

review_title: Great support team, buggy report filters during creation
author: Evonne S.
pros: 1. Super fast and responsive support team!! Thank you so much team. Your support has been a huge role 2. Able to request for customized features 3. Able to schedule emails to be sent automatically however many we want. There are platforms out there that has this feature but they only allow to send 1 email or push per day to reduce congestion
cons: 1. Visualization can be improved. A lot of times they charts are congested and could be more responsive. Eg. Toggles that can adjust the charts instead of reloading the whole report again to get a subset of the data. 2. Creating reports with that tiny frame is a little tough at times when you want to view more of the codes in one screen. Ended up have to scroll a lot 3. Low user customizing-ability. Admin have to create reports and maintain the reports very thoroughly. If users can drag and drop to adjust things themselves, then admin can concentrate more on bringing better value rather than maintaining the reports according to every different users' demand. 4. Report format is quite unreadable in email

review_title: Easy to use with great product support
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Prompt product support team Agile development Easy to integrate
cons: Lack of localization Filtering uses text values

review_title: One-stop solution to common analytics needs
author: Jacob E.
pros: Holistics contains many useful features that allows you to create end to end analytics solution without much coding or technical knowledge (SQL is the only thing you need). It can manage everything from data transport to transform to data visualisation and report delivery.
cons: Lack of APIs is one drawback for more advanced organisations.

review_title: Great BI Platform with reasonable price and continuous development
author: Changhyun L.
pros: Holistics help us reduce time from data to business intelligence. Easily connect with our data warehouse, write SQL, set visualization settings and finished! And also it offers great "push" functionalities (slack, email, google sheet, etc) therefore our team can naturally react to our data.
cons: There is room for further improvement in geospatial related features.

review_title: Holistics is useful esp in my role.
author: Ridhwan M.
pros: The number of reports available or can be created is very useful. On a less peak day, the report can be extracted and dowloaded quite fast.
cons: The long queue when extracting reports can sometimes be a drag especially during peak usage. Sometimes when the report is generated, it takes long for it to be ready for download.

review_title: I like to submit feedback on features and this is responded to and followed up by Holistics Support.
author: Stephani Marcelli D.
pros: - Holistics Support is very responsive. - Holistics always make improvements of its features and transparent about the roadmap to its customers - Holistics is the ultimate BI Tools that provides email reporting schedule feature.
cons: - Loading query is still relatively longer than other BI tools. - Holistics UX less attractive/eye-catching

review_title: Excellent Customer Support
author: Daphne C.
pros: Easy to use and friendly, helpful support. More features are being added to the software, and I'm always keen to see what else the software can do.
cons: Drill-down reports are still not very easy to be implemented. Perhaps there could be more automation in setting up similar email reports to different recipients.

review_title: Holistics
author: Thang N.
pros: - Able to connect to various sources of data - Data visualization: various types, interactive, easy to use - SQL editor & Data Modeling: very useful when connecting our data to Holistics, also easy to use - UI: clean & efficient, current version is well organized
cons: Already contacted Support team when I had improvement ideas. Overall, I'm satisfied with the current software.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Amazing tool with Amazing support
author: Benjamin G.
pros: Dashboard creation is very simple and powerful Integration is smooth Support is really helpful
cons: Some features are still in development for Holistics V3, for certain users looking for very specific features it can be an issue (june 2020) but the support is really helpful to find workaround and come with solutions.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: It is a great tool to use for visualization data
author: Azhar K.
pros: 1. user friendly interface 2. really help for our bussiness needs (I checked and tried all of this kind of services) 3. good price 3. CS is great and fast
cons: Not as fast as one would like, often there is a warning "This page does not respond" and I have to wait
